title: Worsening dyspnea in a 68-year-old smoker
presentation: A 68-year-old male with 50 pack-year smoking history and known COPD presents with 3 days of worsening dyspnea, increased sputum production now yellow-green, and audible wheeze. He uses home oxygen at 2L/min. Vitals: BP 138/86, HR 108, RR 28, Temp 38.1C, SpO2 84% on room air. Exam: barrel chest, prolonged expiration, diffuse expiratory wheeze, using accessory muscles.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Target SpO2 88-92% in COPD — avoid high flow O2 which can worsen hypercapnia. ABG showing hypercapnia + acidosis = NIV (BiPAP). Antibiotics if purulent sputum. NIV failure = intubation.
